title: Stable-GUID + non-destructive update tooling (semver + changelog)
context: Anki's spaced repetition depends on stable card identity across deck updates. If a card's GUID changes between versions, Anki treats it as a new card, destroying the learner's scheduling history. Non-destructive update tooling ensures that GUID stability is enforced, deck versions are properly semver-bumped, and a changelog is maintained so learners and reviewers know what changed.
objective: Build tooling that enforces deterministic, collision-free GUID derivation, validates GUID stability across deck updates, bumps semver, and appends a changelog entry on each update.
acceptanceCriteria: Derives deterministic, collision-free GUIDs from a stable key (e.g., deck-id + note-type + stable card key); same key always produces the same GUID.
On deck update, validates that existing GUIDs are unchanged; rejects any update that would delete or change an existing GUID without an explicit deprecation flag.
Updating a deck preserves GUIDs so learners keep scheduling history; bumps semver (patch/minor/major per change type) and appends a changelog entry.
Provides a CLI command to diff two deck versions and report GUID changes, additions, and removals.
Includes unit tests and a migration guide; licensed MIT.
output: A merged PR adding GUID stability and update tooling (tools/update-deck/) with semver bumping, changelog generation, and a diff command; licensed MIT.
